description: Surf Alerts Frontend - Landing page and dashboard for configuring surf condition text alerts

userStories.title: Initialize Next.js project with dependencies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the frontend project scaffolded with Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind, and shadcn/ui.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/frontend directory with Next.js 14+ App Router
Configure TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
Initialize shadcn/ui with default theme
Set up ocean-inspired color palette (blues/teals) in tailwind.config.ts
Configure Inter font as default sans-serif
Dev server runs with 'npm run dev'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create mock data file with surf spots
userStories.description: As a developer, I need predefined surf spot data and mock user data for the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/mock-data.ts with array of surf spots (name, id, region)
Include at least 15 real Surfline spot names from California
Add mock user object with name, email, phone fields
Add mock alerts array structure
Export TypeScript types for Spot, User, Alert
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create navigation header component
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see navigation with Login/Sign Up buttons so I can access my account.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/landing/header.tsx
Display 'Surf Alerts' brand name/logo placeholder on left
Login and Sign Up buttons on right
Buttons link to /login and /signup routes
Header is sticky and responsive
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create landing page hero section
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to understand what Surf Alerts does within seconds of landing on the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/page.tsx with hero section
Clear headline explaining surf alerts value prop
Subheadline with brief description
Primary CTA button ('Get Started' linking to /signup)
Ocean-themed background or imagery placeholder
Fully responsive (mobile 375px, tablet 768px, desktop 1280px+)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create 'How it Works' section
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see how the alerting system works so I understand the value.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'How it Works' section to landing page below hero
Three-step flow: 1) Pick your spots, 2) Set your threshold, 3) Get text alerts
Each step has icon/illustration placeholder and brief description
Clean grid/flex layout with proper spacing
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create features/benefits section
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see key features so I understand what I get.
acceptanceCriteria: Add features section to landing page
At least 3 feature cards (e.g., Multiple spots, Rating thresholds, Day selection)
Each card has icon placeholder, title, and short description
Modern minimalist card design with subtle shadows
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create login page UI
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a professional login page so I can access my dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/login/page.tsx
Email and password input fields using shadcn/ui Input
Submit button using shadcn/ui Button
Link to signup page ('Don't have an account? Sign up')
Centered card layout with clean styling
Clicking submit navigates to /dashboard (no real auth)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create signup page UI
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a signup page so I can create an account.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/signup/page.tsx
Email, password, and confirm password fields
Submit button
Link to login page ('Already have an account? Log in')
Basic client-side validation (passwords match, email format)
Clicking submit navigates to /dashboard (no real auth)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create mock auth context
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a mock auth context to simulate logged-in state.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/auth-context.tsx with React Context
Provide mock user object (name, email) when 'logged in'
isAuthenticated boolean state
login() and logout() functions that toggle state
Wrap app in AuthProvider
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create dashboard layout with sidebar
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want a dashboard with sidebar navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/dashboard/layout.tsx
Fixed sidebar on left with navigation links
Nav items: Home, Alerts, Settings (phone setup)
Main content area on right
Responsive: sidebar collapses to hamburger menu on mobile
Uses mock auth context to display user name
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create dashboard home page
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want to see a welcome message and alerts summary.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/dashboard/page.tsx
Welcome message with user's name from auth context
Card showing count of active alerts
Quick action button to create new alert
Clean dashboard layout using shadcn/ui Card
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create alerts state management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need state management for alerts CRUD operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/alerts-context.tsx with React Context
Store array of Alert objects in state
Provide addAlert, updateAlert, deleteAlert functions
Initialize with sample mock alerts
Persist to localStorage for session continuity
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create alert card component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my alerts displayed as cards with key info.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/alerts/alert-card.tsx
Display surf spot name prominently
Show minimum rating as stars (1-5)
Show selected days as abbreviated badges (Mon, Tue, etc.)
Edit and Delete icon buttons in card header
Uses shadcn/ui Card component
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create alerts list page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view all my configured alerts in a grid.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/dashboard/alerts/page.tsx
Display grid of AlertCard components
'Add New Alert' button at top
Empty state message when no alerts exist
Responsive grid (1 col mobile, 2 cols tablet, 3 cols desktop)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create alert form modal - spot and rating
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select a surf spot and rating when creating an alert.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/alerts/alert-form-modal.tsx using shadcn/ui Dialog
Dropdown/Select to choose surf spot from mock data list
Each option shows spot name and region
Star rating selector (1-5) for minimum threshold
Clicking stars updates selection visually
Form state managed with useState
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add day selection to alert form
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select which days of the week to receive alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: Add day-of-week checkboxes to alert form modal
Seven checkboxes: Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun
Use shadcn/ui Checkbox components
At least one day must be selected to save
Checkboxes styled as toggle buttons or chips
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add text message preview to alert form
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a preview of what the text message will look like.
acceptanceCriteria: Add preview section to alert form modal
Preview updates live as user changes selections
Show example message: 'Surf Alert: [Spot] is [rating]+ stars today!'
Styled to look like a text message bubble
Preview only shows when spot and rating are selected
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire up alert creation flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to save my alert configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Save button to alert form modal
Clicking Save calls addAlert from alerts context
Modal closes after successful save
New alert appears in alerts list immediately
Form validation: require spot, rating, and at least one day

userStories.title: Add edit functionality for alerts
userStories.description: As a user, I want to edit my existing alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Edit on alert card opens form modal pre-filled with alert data
Modal title changes to 'Edit Alert'
Save button calls updateAlert from context
Changes reflected immediately in alert list

userStories.title: Add delete functionality for alerts
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete alerts with a confirmation step.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Delete on alert card shows confirmation dialog
Use shadcn/ui AlertDialog component
Confirm button calls deleteAlert from context
Cancel button closes dialog without action
Alert removed from list immediately after confirm

userStories.title: Create phone setup settings page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add my mobile phone number for text alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx
Phone number input field with basic format validation
Display currently saved number (from mock context)
Save button that updates mock user context
Success toast/message when number is 'saved'
Use shadcn/ui Input and Button

userStories.title: Add responsive polish and final styling
userStories.description: As a user, I want all pages to look polished on all screen sizes.
acceptanceCriteria: Test and fix any responsive issues on mobile (375px)
Test and fix tablet layout (768px)
Ensure consistent spacing and typography across all pages
Add subtle hover/focus states to interactive elements
Landing page achieves 90+ Lighthouse performance score
